name: Frontend Loose Ends: Type Sweep + Schedule Chart Overlay
description: Two focused frontend stories closing the loose ends from the hourly-overrides batch before the user takes the plugin to a real Home Assistant for testing. (1) Type sweep: a shared `expandHourlyTo48` helper, tightened `source` literal unions on schedule/rates response types, and small `hasHourlyComfortBands` / `hasCustomRates` guards so consumers stop writing inline null checks. (2) Schedule chart overlay: `<hm-schedule-chart>` gains optional `comfortHighs` / `comfortLows` props rendered as a translucent band behind the optimizer's setpoints, and the dashboard panel fetches preferences so HVAC appliances visually show 'what the user allowed' alongside 'what the optimizer chose'. Both stories are pure-additive — no behavior change for users without hourly overrides.
env_policy: Frontend-only batch. All tests use vitest + happy-dom + mocked fetch. No backend or live-env work in scope. All Verify steps run offline via npm run build, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, and grep.

stories.title: Type sweep + shared hourly-expansion helper + override guards
stories.description: As the frontend codebase, I need one canonical 24→48 expansion helper, tighter literal-union types on `source` fields, and small guard functions so any future surface that consumes hourly overrides shares the same primitives.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `base-frontend/src/utils/hourly.ts` exporting two functions: `expandHourlyTo48(arr: number[]): number[]` — given a 24-element array, return a 48-element array where index 2*i and 2*i+1 both equal arr[i]; throws `RangeError('expandHourlyTo48 expects exactly 24 values, got <n>')` for any other length. `collapse48ToHourly(arr: number[]): number[]` — given a 48-element array, return a 24-element array sampled at even indices (arr[0], arr[2], ..., arr[46]); throws `RangeError` for any other length. The collapse helper is the inverse used when seeding the editor from a 48-element zone curve.

Tighten `source` to a literal union where the API contract is fixed: in `base-frontend/src/api/schedules.ts`, change `HvacScheduleResponse.source` and `ApplianceScheduleEntry.source` from `string` to `'optimization' | 'defaults'`; in `base-frontend/src/api/appliances.ts`, change `ApplianceSchedule.source` from `string` to `'optimization' | 'defaults'`. Confirm `RatesResponse.source` is already `'custom' | 'zone'` (no change). No runtime change — these are pure compile-time tightenings.

Add to `base-frontend/src/utils/hourly.ts` two boolean guards: `hasHourlyComfortBands(prefs: { hourly_high_temps_f?: number[] | null; hourly_low_temps_f?: number[] | null }): boolean` returning true iff both arrays are non-null AND length 24; `hasCustomRates(rates: { source: 'custom' | 'zone' }): boolean` returning `rates.source === 'custom'`.

Refactor existing call sites that already do these checks inline to use the new guards: `base-frontend/src/ui/constraint-editor.ts` (where it decides whether to pre-check the 'Use my hourly bands' checkbox) and `base-frontend/src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts` settings rates section (where it decides whether to show the 'Clear override' button). Refactor must preserve existing behavior exactly — just substitute the guard call.

Create `base-frontend/tests/hourly.test.ts` covering: (a) `expandHourlyTo48([0..23])` returns a 48-element array where `result[0] === 0`, `result[1] === 0`, `result[2] === 1`, ..., `result[46] === 23`, `result[47] === 23`; (b) `expandHourlyTo48([])` and `expandHourlyTo48([1,2,3])` both throw `RangeError`; (c) `collapse48ToHourly` is the round-trip inverse of `expand` for a constant-per-hour array; (d) `hasHourlyComfortBands` returns true for both arrays at length 24, false when either is null/undefined or wrong length; (e) `hasCustomRates({source:'custom'})` is true and `hasCustomRates({source:'zone'})` is false.

Verify: `cd base-frontend && npm test` passes (every test in hourly.test.ts green, no regression in existing tests); `cd base-frontend && npx tsc --noEmit` exits clean (the tightened literal unions cause no new errors); `cd base-frontend && npm run build` succeeds; `grep -E "source: 'optimization' \| 'defaults'" base-frontend/src/api/schedules.ts base-frontend/src/api/appliances.ts | wc -l` returns at least 3; `grep -E 'expandHourlyTo48|hasHourlyComfortBands|hasCustomRates' base-frontend/src/ui/constraint-editor.ts base-frontend/src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts | wc -l` returns at least 2 (both call sites refactored).
stories.notes: Created src/utils/hourly.ts exporting expandHourlyTo48 (with RangeError on wrong length), collapse48ToHourly (inverse, samples even indices, RangeError on wrong length), hasHourlyComfortBands (length-24 guard), and hasCustomRates (source==='custom'). Tightened HvacScheduleResponse.source, ApplianceScheduleEntry.source, and ApplianceSchedule.source to literal union 'optimization' | 'defaults' (RatesResponse.source already 'custom' | 'zone'). Refactored constraint-editor._seedHourlyBands to call hasHourlyComfortBands (kept a small inline finite-number filter so non-finite values still fall through to defaults — preserves prior behavior). Refactored panel custom-rates section to call hasCustomRates(rates) where it decides whether to render the 'Clear override' button. Added tests/hourly.test.ts with 14 cases covering all guards/helpers + RangeError messages. All 67 frontend tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build green; verify greps return 3 and 4 as required.

stories.title: Schedule chart shows user comfort bands as translucent overlay
stories.description: As a user with hourly comfort bands set, I want to see my allowed range as a translucent band behind the optimizer's chosen setpoints on every HVAC schedule chart, so I can visually confirm the optimizer is working within my constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `base-frontend/src/ui/schedule-chart.ts` with two new optional reactive properties: `comfortHighs?: number[]` and `comfortLows?: number[]` — each a 48-element float array. When both are set and have length 48, render a translucent band on the SVG: same x-range as the existing highTemps/lowTemps band, but rendered FIRST (z-order behind), with `fill-opacity: 0.25` and stroke `--hm-accent` (amber) at 0.5 opacity. When the optimizer's `highTemps`/`lowTemps` are also passed, the comfort overlay renders behind them so the user sees both at once.
If only `comfortHighs` and `comfortLows` are passed (no optimizer setpoints), the overlay still renders standalone — handles the 'no optimization yet' case gracefully.

Length mismatch handling: if `comfortHighs.length !== 48` or `comfortLows.length !== 48`, log a single `console.warn` (using the existing `_lastWarnedLength` de-duplication pattern from US-FE-06 — extend it to cover comfort arrays too) and skip the overlay; do NOT crash. The rest of the chart renders normally.

Extend `base-frontend/src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts`: when fetching `/api/v1/schedules` + `/api/v1/rates` for the dashboard view, also fetch `/api/v1/preferences` in parallel via `preferencesApi.get()`. Cache alongside the others. On error of the preferences call, dashboard still renders (preferences fetch is non-blocking — fall through to no overlay).

When passing schedule data to `<hm-schedule-chart>` for HVAC appliances, also pass `comfortHighs` + `comfortLows`: if `hasHourlyComfortBands(prefs)` (using the guard from US-FE-SWEEP-01), expand both 24-element arrays to 48 via `expandHourlyTo48` and pass them; otherwise omit. Non-HVAC appliance types do not get comfort overlays — keep them as-is.

Add a small legend below the appliance card when the overlay renders: a 12x12 swatch in the amber translucent color + the text 'Your comfort range'. Only shown when overlay is active. CSS via the existing tokens stylesheet.

Tests in `base-frontend/tests/schedule-chart.test.ts` (extend the existing file): (a) mounting `<hm-schedule-chart>` with `comfortHighs=[70]*48, comfortLows=[68]*48` renders an SVG element with `fill-opacity` (or class) corresponding to the translucent comfort band; (b) mounting with `comfortHighs.length=24` (wrong length) does NOT render the overlay and emits a warn; (c) mounting with `highTemps=[74]*48, lowTemps=[70]*48, comfortHighs=[78]*48, comfortLows=[66]*48` renders both bands and the comfort band's elements come BEFORE the setpoints band's elements in document order (assert via querySelectorAll order).

Tests in `base-frontend/tests/panel-schedules.test.ts` (extend existing): (d) mock fetch responses include `/api/v1/preferences` with `hourly_high_temps_f: [74]*24, hourly_low_temps_f: [70]*24` and an HVAC appliance — assert the rendered `<hm-schedule-chart>` has `comfortHighs` and `comfortLows` properties set with length-48 arrays where every value is 74 / 70 respectively; (e) mock with preferences NOT set (both fields null) — assert chart's `comfortHighs` is undefined; (f) mock preferences fetch returning a 500 — assert dashboard still renders the chart without overlay (graceful degradation).

Verify: `cd base-frontend && npm test` passes (all new tests green, no regression in existing schedule-chart or panel-schedules tests); `cd base-frontend && npm run build` succeeds; `grep -E 'comfortHighs|comfortLows' base-frontend/src/ui/schedule-chart.ts base-frontend/src/panel/hungry-machines-panel.ts | wc -l` returns at least 4 (declared on chart + passed by panel); `grep -oE 'Your comfort range' base-frontend/dist/hungry-machines.js | head -1` matches.
stories.notes: Extended hm-schedule-chart with optional comfortHighs/comfortLows props (length-48 number arrays). Renders translucent .comfort-band <rect>s in amber (--hm-accent, fill-opacity 0.25 + stroke at 0.5 opacity) BEFORE the existing .temp-band rects so the optimizer's setpoints sit visually on top. Added a separate _lastWarnedComfortLength dedupe field that mirrors the existing _lastWarnedLength pattern; if either comfort array is provided but the pair isn't both length-48, logs one console.warn and skips just the overlay (rest of the chart still renders). overlayValues now includes comfort temps so scaleY accommodates a comfort-only chart. Panel side: added _preferences state, imported preferences API + utils/hourly guards, fetches /api/v1/preferences in parallel with schedules/rates/appliances via getPreferences().catch(() => null) so a 500 degrades gracefully. _renderApplianceCard, only for type==='hvac' and when hasHourlyComfortBands(prefs) is true, expands hourly_high_temps_f / hourly_low_temps_f via expandHourlyTo48 and passes them to <hm-schedule-chart>. Non-HVAC appliances skip comfort entirely. Added a small legend below the chart (12x12 amber swatch + 'Your comfort range' label) only when comfort arrays exist. Tests: schedule-chart.test.ts extended with 3 cases (renders 48 comfort rects on length-48 inputs; warns + no rects on length-24 mismatch; comfort-band rect appears before temp-band rect in document order); panel-schedules.test.ts extended with a new describe block of 3 cases (length-24 prefs → length-48 chart props all 74/70 + 'Your comfort range' present on hvac card only; null prefs → undefined comfort props + no legend; /preferences 500 → dashboard still renders, charts have undefined comfort props, legend absent). All 73 frontend tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean; npm run build green; grep -E 'comfortHighs|comfortLows' on chart+panel returns 25 (>=4 required); 'Your comfort range' present in dist bundle.
